body.title: Active inhibition propagation through graph
body.result: BG inhibition propagation wired: _inhibit_neighbors() in basal_ganglia.py writes direction=inhibition edges from winner to graph-connected near-misses; set_cortex() injected at boot from main.py. Graceful no-op until graph has edges. Syntax OK.
body.description: When a node wins (habit fires, NE selects a frame), propagate suppression to competing hot nodes via graph edges. Currently BG lateral inhibition only affects habit scoring; this extends suppression into the graph itself — winners cool their semantic competition. Implementation: after habit dispatch or NE frame selection, identify winning node(s), walk neighbors, apply heat penalty proportional to edge weight and activation overlap. Prereq: T-spreading-activation.
